SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (SRS)
Project Title: Customer Sentiment Analysis on Flipkart Product Reviews

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document provides a comprehensive overview of the Requirements Specification for the Customer Sentiment Analysis system. It describes the data pipeline, processing frameworks, and visual tracking requirements used to understand buyer behavior on Flipkart.

1.2 Scope
The scope of this system involves processing text-based consumer reviews and categorical ratings extracted from Flipkart. It implements text processing algorithms to compute consumer sentiment scores and classifies them into distinct operational buckets (Positive, Negative, Neutral).

2. SYSTEM OVERVIEW & REQUIRMENTS
2.1 Functional Requirements
- Data Ingestion: The system must successfully accept data inputs via flat files (.csv) containing structural columns: Unnamed: 0, Product_name, Review, and Rating.
- Text Preprocessing: System must automate lower-casing, removal of non-alphabetic special characters/emojis, and removal of English grammar stopwords.
- Sentiment Modeling: System must utilize Natural Language Processing (NLP) tools to calculate polarity parameters and map them to sentiment metrics.
- Visual Reporting: System must accurately render data distributions using graphical charts (Bar charts and Stacked distribution charts).

2.2 Non-Functional Requirements
- Accuracy: Polarity metrics should align meaningfully with user-provided star-ratings (e.g., 5-star ratings should highly correlate with Positive sentiment).
- Performance & Scalability: Data processing execution time must remain minimal (processing batches of ~2300 entries in under 5 seconds).

3. SYSTEM DATA ARCHITECTURE
3.1 Dataset Schema
- Unnamed: 0 (int64): Database auto-incremented index token.
- Product_name (String): Identity designation text of the item sold.
- Review (String): Descriptive literal user experience assessment text.
- Rating (int64): Numeric quantitative satisfaction marker bounded between 1 to 5 stars.
